name: Hepatitis C - Diagnosis Code Query (Acute + Chronic)
description: Tests ability to find hepatitis C virus (HCV) infection patients via diagnosis codes. HCV is documented under multiple SNOMED codes by acuity: Viral hepatitis C (parent), Acute hepatitis C, Chronic hepatitis C. Most diagnosed cases are chronic (>80%) since acute HCV is often asymptomatic and undetected. Synthea distributes patients across these codes. An LLM querying only the parent will miss patients coded with the acuity-specific subtypes.
prompt: Find all patients with a diagnosis of hepatitis C virus infection, including acute and chronic forms.

prompts.naive: Find all patients with hepatitis C.
prompts.broad: Find all patients diagnosed with hepatitis C virus (HCV) infection — including the parent viral hepatitis C concept, acute hepatitis C, and chronic hepatitis C. Most diagnosed HCV is chronic.
prompts.expert: I need to identify all hepatitis C patients. HCV is documented under multiple SNOMED codes by acuity: Viral hepatitis type C (50711007) is the parent, with subtype variants Chronic hepatitis C (128302006) and Acute hepatitis C (235866006). The CSTE 'Acute Hepatitis C Virus Infection (Disorders)' VSAC (OID 2.16.840.1.113762.1.4.1146.2679) documents the acute codes. Search Condition resources for all three codes.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with HCV diagnosis codes (Paths A and B). The parent + acuity subtypes pattern is common for infectious diseases.
